title: 65W USB-C PD Adapter
description: A quasi-resonant flyback converter converting the bridge-rectified AC mains bus (100–370 V DC) to a 20 V/3.25 A output, forming the power stage of a 65 W USB-C Power Delivery charger.
design_notes: The input range of 100–370 V represents the full-wave rectified DC bus from 90–264 Vac mains without power-factor correction (bulk capacitor output). A complete PD adapter adds an active PFC stage in front, which raises this to a regulated ~400 V DC bus — in that case, set Vin to 380–400 V. The 5 % leakage inductance ratio is typical for a practical flyback transformer with separated primary/secondary windings; the RCD clamp dissipates approximately 1.5–2 W, which must be budgeted into thermal design. USB-C PD output voltage negotiation (5/9/12/15/20 V) is implemented by a PD controller (e.g. FUSB302, CYPD3135) adjusting the feedback reference — this power stage is designed at the 20 V maximum PDO. The 100 kHz switching frequency is chosen to comfortably fit within the quasi-resonant ZVS valley-switching window at light load.
source: TI SLUA107B — Flyback Converter Design; ON Semiconductor AND9135; USB PD Spec Rev 3.1
